findings.evidence: `noncomputable def prodChar` (public); sibling file Product.lean:80 `private noncomputable def restrictHom`. Docstring 'Main definitions' omits prodChar.
findings.issue: prodChar (and prodChar_apply) is an implementation helper used only to build prodTensorAlgHom via lift; it is exposed but absent from the docstring's 'Main definitions'. The parallel helper in Product.lean (restrictHom) is private.

findings.evidence: prodTensorAlgEquiv body: `AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom (prodTensorAlgHom R) (tensorProdAlgHom R)`; only the equivs appear in the 'Main definitions' list and in DiagonalizableGroup downstream.
findings.fix: Make prodTensorAlgHom, tensorProdAlgHom, and their *_single simp lemmas `private`; keep prodTensorAlgEquiv/prodTensorBialgEquiv public.
findings.issue: prodTensorAlgHom and tensorProdAlgHom are the two halves bundled into prodTensorAlgEquiv via AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom; nothing downstream references them directly and they are not in 'Main definitions'. Exposing both one-directional maps plus the equiv over-widens the surface.

findings.evidence: Only forward components (prodPointsMulEquiv_fst/snd_ofConv_single) are provided; the symm direction needed to use the equiv as an iso is uncharacterized.
findings.fix: Add a simp lemma characterizing (prodPointsMulEquiv.symm (f₁,f₂)).ofConv (single (g,h) 1) = f₁.ofConv (single g 1) * f₂.ofConv (single h 1).
findings.issue: prodPointsMulEquiv is exposed as a headline isomorphism but its inverse direction has no characteristic lemma: there is no way to compute the point of D(G×H) built from a pair (f₁,f₂) on the generator single (g,h) 1 without unfolding.

summary: The bialgebra and points equivalences and their characteristic lemmas are correct and well-annotated, but the file leaks three pieces of internal scaffolding (prodChar and the two one-directional algebra maps) as public, contrary to its own 'Main definitions' list and the private-helper convention of the sibling Product.lean.
